6. RAG System Architecture 
The company uses Retrieval-Augmented Generation systems for internal document search. 
The RAG pipeline includes: 
1. Document ingestion 
2. Text extraction 
3. Chunking 
4. Embedding generation 
5. Vector storage 
6. Similarity retrieval 
7. LLM response generation 
The vector database currently used is FAISS for local development and Pinecone for cloud 
production systems.  
7. Chunk

currently used is FAISS for local development and Pinecone for cloud 
production systems.  
7. Chunking Strategy 
The AI team uses overlapping chunking for document processing. 
Default configuration: 
• Chunk size: 500 characters 
• Overlap: 100 characters 
The purpose of overlap is to preserve context between chunks. 
Example: 
If one chunk ends with a sentence and another chunk starts immediately after it, overlap 
ensures the sentence meaning is not lost.

9. Vector Databases 
The engineering department works with several vector databases. 
FAISS 
A local vector similarity library created by Meta. 
Advantages: 
• very fast 
• lightweight 
• good for local projects 
Disadvantages: 
• not ideal for distributed cloud scaling 
Pinecone 
A managed cloud vector database. 
Advantages: 
• scalable 
• production-ready 
• metadata filtering 
Disadvant

cloud vector database. 
Advantages: 
• scalable 
• production-ready 
• metadata filtering 
Disadvantages: 
• paid service 
 
10. Authentication System 
The backend authentication system uses JWT tokens. 
Authentication flow: 
1. User logs in 
2. Server validates credentials 
3. JWT token generated 
4. Frontend stores token 
5. Protected routes verify token Refresh tokens are used for long sessions. 
Passwords are hashed using bcrypt before storage.

13. Deployment Process 
Applications are deployed using Docker containers. Deployment flow:

2. GitHub Actions run tests 
3. Docker image is built 
4. Image deployed to cloud server 
Production servers are hosted on AWS.

14. Security Policies 
Employees must:

Sensitive data should never be exposed in logs. 
 
15. Frequently Asked Questions 
Q1: What is RAG? 
RAG stands for Retrieval-Augmented Generation. It combines information retrieval with 
language generation. 
Q2: What is an embedding? 
An embedding is a numerical representation of text that captures semantic meaning. 
Q3: Why use ch

An embedding is a numerical representation of text that captures semantic meaning. 
Q3: Why use chunk overlap? 
Overlap prevents important contextual information from being lost between chunks. 
Q4: What is FAISS? 
FAISS is a vector similarity search library used for fast semantic retrieval. 
Q5: What is semantic search? Semantic search finds information based on meaning rather than exact keywords.

16. AI Chatbot Guidelines 
The chatbot should: 
• avoid hallucinations 
• answer only from provided context 
• provide concise responses 
• mention uncertainty when information is missing 
The chatbot must prioritize retrieved context over assumptions.
